title: FEMS bitemporal indexing: valid_from/valid_until + recorded_at/invalidated_at on MemoryItem (folded WP-1-FEMS-Bitemporal-Indexing)
goal: Add bi-temporal timestamps to FEMS V1 MemoryItem so MemoryCapsule replay can reconstruct exactly which memories existed AND were considered valid at a given instant. Ports the Graphiti/Zep temporal knowledge graph pattern. Folded from WP-1-FEMS-Bitemporal-Indexing into KERNEL-004 cluster D.3.
expected_diff_shape: Extends src/backend/handshake_core/src/kernel/fems_mt_handoff_memory_context.rs (in main) with bi-temporal MemoryItem fields. New module src/backend/handshake_core/src/memory/bitemporal.rs (BitemporalIndex + AsOfQuery + BitemporalError types). Postgres migration adds `valid_from`, `valid_until`, `recorded_at`, `invalidated_at` columns. Tests src/backend/handshake_core/tests/bitemporal_indexing_tests.rs.
unblock_conditions: MT-141 (MemoryCapsule), MT-143 (CapsuleBuilder). FEMS V1 surfaces in main.
implementation_notes: Per WP-1-FEMS-Bitemporal-Indexing preserved_intent: 'Add bi-temporal timestamps to MemoryItem - valid_from/valid_until (when the fact was true in the world) and recorded_at/invalidated_at (when we recorded/invalidated it). Ports the Graphiti/Zep temporal knowledge graph pattern.' Surface: extends MemoryItem with `{ pub valid_from: DateTime<Utc>, pub valid_until: Option<DateTime<Utc>>, pub recorded_at: DateTime<Utc>, pub invalidated_at: Option<DateTime<Utc>> }`. New struct `pub struct AsOfQuery { as_of_world_time: DateTime<Utc>, as_of_recorded_time: DateTime<Utc> }`. BitemporalIndex provides `pub fn items_visible_at(query: &AsOfQuery) -> Result<Vec<MemoryItem>, BitemporalError>` which filters items where `valid_from <= as_of_world_time < (valid_until OR infinity) AND recorded_at <= as_of_recorded_time < (invalidated_at OR infinity)`. Postgres migration: ADD COLUMN valid_from TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(), valid_until TIMESTAMPTZ NULL, recorded_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(), invalidated_at TIMESTAMPTZ NULL; CREATE INDEX ON memory_item (valid_from, valid_until); CREATE INDEX ON memory_item (recorded_at, invalidated_at). Backfill defaults existing rows so the migration is non-destructive. Tests: items_visible_at correctly filters across both dimensions; a memory invalidated at T1 is visible for queries with recorded_at < T1 but not >= T1; a memory with valid_until in the future is visible for queries within its valid window; replay reconstruction returns identical MemoryPack for two queries with same (world_time, recorded_time) pair.
risk_if_missed: Without bi-temporal indexing, replay cannot distinguish 'memory existed but was superseded' from 'memory didn't exist yet'; outcome feedback (MT-158) cannot attribute outcomes to the exact pack composition.
validator_focus: Both temporal axes (world time + recorded time) honored independently. Migration is non-destructive. Indexes cover the common query shape.

minimum_controls: Migration is non-destructive; existing rows backfilled with sane defaults.
Indexes added for the common (valid_from, valid_until) and (recorded_at, invalidated_at) query patterns.
AsOfQuery exposes both axes; callers cannot accidentally collapse to single-temporal queries.
